name: Chicken & Red Bell Pepper Bolognese
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chardonnay or other dry white wine
1 tbsp Fresh basil leaves, finely shredded
1 tbsp Fresh Italian parsley, finely chopped
1 t Fresh rosemary leaves, finely chopped
2 medium Red bell peppers, roasted, peeled, stemmed, seeded, and coarsely chopped (save juices)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan or skillet, heat the olive oil with the onion and garlic over moderate heat.
2. When they sizzle, add the chicken and sauté it, stirring and breaking up the meat with a wooden spoon until it begins to brown (5-7 minutes).
3. Add the wine and stir, deglazing the pan.
4. Add the tomatoes, breaking them up with your hands, and stir in the remaining ingredients.
5. Simmer the sauce until thick but still slightly liquid, 20 to 25 minutes.
6. Spoon over cooked pasta (spaghetti, fettuccine, or medium-sized tubular shells or other shapes are recommended).
Directions for roasting bell peppers:
1. Place the peppers in a foil-lined baking sheet in an oven set to 500 degrees.
2. Roast until their skins are evenly blistered and browned, about 25 minutes (turning them 2-3 times so they roast evenly).
3. Remove from the oven and cover with a kitchen towel.
4. When cool enough to handle, pull out stems, peel away blackened skins, open peppers, and remove seeds using a teaspoon.
5. Carefully pour through a fine mesh strainer to remove any seeds or bits of skin, saving flavorful juices as desired.
